extra.message:
Read Permission Defined at Workflow Level

The 'contents: read' permission is defined at the workflow level instead of the job level. Workflow-level permissions apply to all jobs, violating the principle of least privilege.

Impact: Over-privileged jobs — all jobs in the workflow receive this permission even if they don't need it, increasing the blast radius of a compromised job.

Fix: Move the 'contents: read' permission from the workflow-level permissions block to the specific job that requires it.

Write Permission Defined at Workflow Level (pages)

The 'pages: write' permission is defined at the workflow level instead of the job level. Workflow-level permissions apply to all jobs, violating the principle of least privilege.

Impact: Over-privileged jobs — all jobs in the workflow receive write access to GitHub Pages even if they don't need it, increasing the blast radius of a compromised job.

Fix: Move the 'pages: write' permission from the workflow-level permissions block to the specific job that requires it.

Write Permission Defined at Workflow Level (id-token)

The 'id-token: write' permission is defined at the workflow level instead of the job level. Workflow-level permissions apply to all jobs, violating the principle of least privilege.

Impact: Over-privileged jobs — all jobs in the workflow receive the ability to request OIDC tokens even if they don't need it, increasing the blast radius of a compromised job.

Fix: Move the 'id-token: write' permission from the workflow-level permissions block to the specific job that requires it.

XML External Entity (XXE) Injection via libxmljs

XML parsing uses libxmljs.parseXmlString(rawXml, { noent: true, dtdload: true }) which explicitly enables entity resolution (noent: true) and DTD loading (dtdload: true), allowing XXE attacks.

Impact: Arbitrary file read, SSRF, potential denial of service via billion laughs attack.

Fix: Disable external entity processing: use { noent: false, dtdload: false } or switch to a safe XML parser.
